feedback.markdownBody:
# Verdict: APPROVED

## Summary
PR #1058 (PAN-1044) introduces the Command Deck Project Overview Panel ‚Äî a hero billboard, pipeline swimlanes, per-issue cost badge with hover breakdown, and click-split routing for `ProjectNode`. Round 1 identified one High-severity correctness defect: stuck features were rendered in both `StuckCallout` and a general `PipelineSection`, producing duplicate cards for any project with stuck issues. Five follow-up commits resolved the defect by typing `PIPELINE_STAGES` as `Exclude<PipelineStage, 'stuck'>[]` (compile-time guard), separating it from a new `BUCKET_STAGES` array used only for Map initialization and `activeStageCount`, and adding a regression test with `queryAllByText(...).toHaveLength(1)` and `queryByRole('region', { name: 'Stuck / blocked pipeline stage' }).not.toBeInTheDocument()` assertions. All 34 acceptance criteria are verified across all six vBRIEF items; the security pass is clean; the four performance observations are all advisory nits with no measurable impact at current project scale. The PR is approved for merge.

## Blockers (MUST fix before merge)

_none_

## High Priority (SHOULD fix; synthesis may still approve if justified)

_none_

## Nits (advisory ‚Äî safe to defer)

- `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ProjectOverview.tsx:127` ‚Äî `?` ‚Äî Broad Zustand selector subscribes to the entire `reviewStatusByIssueId` map, re-rendering `ProjectOverview` on every domain event touching any issue's review status. Consider `useShallow` scoped to the current project's features if project counts grow large. (performance)
- `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ProjectOverview.tsx:418` ‚Äî `?` ‚Äî `IssueCard` closure `() => onSelectFeature(entry.feature)` is recreated on every render. Zero current impact; only becomes relevant if `IssueCard` is later wrapped in `React.memo`. (performance)
- `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ProjectOverview.tsx:158‚Äì159` ‚Äî `?` ‚Äî `stuckFeatures` and `activeStageCount` computed outside `useMemo`; O(9) constant cost per render. The `?? []` fallback on `stuckFeatures` is dead code (all stages pre-populated by the init loop). Trivially safe to defer indefinitely. (performance)
- `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ProjectOverview.tsx:565‚Äì566` ‚Äî `?` ‚Äî `sortedCostRows` allocates on hover (`Object.entries` + `.sort`, k ‚â§ 5 rows). Triggered only on `mouseEnter`, immeasurable in practice. (performance)

## Cross-cutting groups

_none_

## What's good
- Round-1 C-1 fix types `PIPELINE_STAGES` as `Exclude<PipelineStage, 'stuck'>[]` ‚Äî TypeScript now makes it a compile error to re-introduce the duplication, providing durable protection.
- `BUCKET_STAGES` (includes `'stuck'`) feeds `activeStageCount` so the hero "Pipeline stages" stat correctly counts the stuck bucket as an active stage.
- All 34 acceptance criteria across all six vBRIEF items verified; requirements pass is complete and clean.
- Security pass is clean: no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, no `eval`, no user-controlled data reaching any CSS or DOM injection sink, no new trust boundaries.
- `bucketFeature()` correctly uses `session.type === 'work'` (not `role`), honouring the PRD's explicit codebase correction.
- Four-way routing (conversation ‚Üí feature ‚Üí project ‚Üí empty) implemented, tested, and priority-correct per spec.
- All expensive derivations (`bucketedFeatures`, `issueCostDetails`, `selectedProjectData`) correctly wrapped in `useMemo`; all new callbacks in `useCallback`.
- No hardcoded hex or RGB colors anywhere ‚Äî every value uses CSS custom properties throughout.
- Regression test guards the exact failure mode with both a `queryAllByText` count assertion and a `queryByRole` not-in-DOM assertion.
- Cost breakdown tooltip follows the existing `FeatureItem.tsx` hover pattern (onMouseEnter / onMouseLeave / onFocus / onBlur) with graceful degradation when cost data is absent.

## Review stats
- Blockers: 0   High: 0   Medium: 0   Nits: 4
- By reviewer: correctness=0, security=0, performance=4, requirements=0
- Files touched: 19   Files with findings: 1

## Appendix: individual reviews

See individual reviewer output files listed in `## Reviewer Output Files` in the Synthesis Context above. Those files contain full per-reviewer detail; this synthesis is the policy layer.

## ‚úÖ CODE APPROVED ‚Äî YOUR WORK IS COMPLETE

**Do NOT make any more changes.**
**Do NOT run `pan done` again.**
**Do NOT run `pan review request`.**

The specialist pipeline will now run tests. If tests pass, the issue enters the merge queue for human approval.
